revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '旧小川町西部に位置する前方後方墳。 昭和49年に調査され、画文帯四獣鏡が発見された。 周囲は駒形公園として整備。'
revision.text: 旧小川町西部に位置する前方後方墳。 昭和49年に調査され、画文帯四獣鏡が発見された。 周囲は駒形公園として整備。
{{db|定義未満}}旧小川町西部に位置する前方後方墳。 昭和49年に調査され、画文帯四獣鏡が発見された。 周囲は駒形公園として整備。

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=駒形大塚古墳 |画像= |所在地=栃木県那須郡那珂川町小川 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|45|42|N|140|7|14|E|scale:20000}} |形状=[[前方後方墳]] |規模=全長61m |埋葬部=木炭槨 木棺 |築造年代=4世紀中期 |被葬者= |出土品= |史跡指定=国史跡（1979年3月13日） |特記事項= }}
'''駒形大塚古墳'''（こまがたおおつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[那珂川町 (栃木県)|那珂川町]]（旧[[小川町 (栃木県)|小川町]]域西部）に位置する[[前方後方墳]]。
== 概要 == [[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）に[[発掘調査]]がなされ、画文帯四獣鏡が発見された。
[[1979年]]（昭和54年）[[3月13日]]、「'''那須小川古墳群'''」として、吉田温泉神社古墳群、那須八幡塚古墳群とともに一括して国の[[史跡]]に指定された。
現在、周囲は駒形公園として整備されている。

revision.comment: /* 概要 */ 規模・出土品
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=駒形大塚古墳 |画像= |所在地=栃木県那須郡那珂川町小川 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|45|42|N|140|7|14|E|scale:20000}} |形状=[[前方後方墳]] |規模=全長61m 標高132.5m 幅28m |埋葬部=木炭槨 木棺 |築造年代=4世紀中葉 |被葬者=梅曽の政府主であった韋提その人か、その系譜につながる国造の墓（推定） |出土品=鏡・鉄剣・鉄鏃・土器・刀子（小刀）・鉄斧・直刀・鉇 など |史跡指定=国の史跡（1979年3月13日指定） |特記事項=那須小川古墳群のひとつ }}
'''駒形大塚古墳'''（こまがたおおつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[那珂川町 (栃木県)|那珂川町]]（旧[[小川町 (栃木県)|小川町]]域西部）に位置する[[前方後方墳]]。
== 概要 == [[前方後円墳]]出現前の古墳である。
[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）に[[発掘調査]]が行われ、舶載鏡の画文帯四獣鏡一面、銅族5本、直刀二口、鉄斧一個、鉇（やりがんな）一個、ガラス小玉数十個が出土した。前方部は一部削平されているが、後方部はほぼ原形をとどめている。全長64メートル、後方部長さ32メートル、幅約30メートル、高さ8メートル、前方部幅約16メートルである。後方部の中央の地表下から長さ3.2メートル、幅75セントメートルの木炭槨が確認され、同規模の[[割竹形木棺]]が使われたと推定されている。
墳丘には[[葺石]]が葺かれていた。後円部墳頂から古い様式の土師器が多数出土している。
[[1979年]]（昭和54年）[[3月13日]]、「'''那須小川古墳群'''」として、本古墳と同形の吉田温泉神社古墳、那須八幡塚古墳とともに一括して国の[[史跡]]に指定された。
現在、周囲は駒形公園として整備されている。
== 関連項目 == * [[関東の史跡一覧]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[前方後方墳]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index.asp 国指定文化財等データベース]
{{DEFAULTSORT:こまかたおおつか こふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:栃木県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:古墳時代]] [[Category:那珂川町 (栃木県)]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

[[1979年]]（昭和54年）[[3月13日]]、「'''那須小川古墳群'''」として、本古墳と同形の吉田温泉神社古墳、那須八幡塚古墳とともに一括して国の[[史跡]]に指定された。

{{DEFAULTSORT:こまかたおおつか こふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:栃木県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:那珂川町 (栃木県)]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{日本の古墳 |名称=駒形大塚古墳 |画像= |所在地=[[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町 (栃木県)|那珂川町]]小川 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|45|42|N|140|7|14|E|scale:20000}} |形状=[[前方後方墳]] |規模=全長61m 標高132.5m 幅28m |埋葬部=木炭槨 木棺 |築造年代=4世紀中葉 |被葬者=梅曽の政府主であった韋提その人か、その系譜につながる国造の墓（推定） |出土品=鏡・鉄剣・鉄鏃・土器・刀子（小刀）・鉄斧・直刀・鉇 など |史跡指定=国の史跡（1979年3月13日指定） |特記事項=那須小川古墳群のひとつ }}
'''駒形大塚古墳'''（こまがたおおつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町 (栃木県)|那珂川町]]小川に位置する[[前方後方墳]]。
== 概要 == [[前方後円墳]]出現前の古墳である。
[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）に[[発掘調査]]が行われ、舶載鏡の画文帯四獣鏡一面、銅族5本、直刀二口、鉄斧一個、鉇（やりがんな）一個、ガラス小玉数十個が出土した。前方部は一部削平されているが、後方部はほぼ原形をとどめている。全長64メートル、後方部長さ32メートル、幅約30メートル、高さ8メートル、前方部幅約16メートルである。後方部の中央の地表下から長さ3.2メートル、幅75セントメートルの木炭槨が確認され、同規模の[[割竹形木棺]]が使われたと推定されている。
墳丘には[[葺石]]が葺かれていた。後円部墳頂から古い様式の土師器が多数出土している。
[[1979年]]（昭和54年）[[3月13日]]、「'''那須小川古墳群'''」として、本古墳と同形の吉田温泉神社古墳、那須八幡塚古墳とともに一括して国の[[史跡]]に指定された。
現在、周囲は駒形公園として整備されている。

{{日本の古墳 |名称=駒形大塚古墳 |画像= |所在地=[[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町 (栃木県)|那珂川町]]小川 |所属=[[那須小川古墳群]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|45|42|N|140|7|14|E|region:JP-09_scale:20000}} |形状=[[前方後方墳]] |規模=全長61[[メートル|m]] 標高132.5m 幅28m |埋葬部=木炭槨 [[木棺]] |築造年代=4世紀中葉 |被葬者=梅曽の政府主であった韋提その人か、その系譜につながる[[国造]]の墓（推定） |出土品=鏡・鉄剣・[[鉄鏃]]・[[土器]]・[[刀子]]（小刀）・鉄斧・[[直刀]]・鉇 など |史跡指定=国の[[史跡]]（1979年3月13日指定） |特記事項= }}
'''駒形大塚古墳'''（こまがたおおつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町 (栃木県)|那珂川町]]小川に位置する[[前方後方墳]]。
== 概要 == [[前方後円墳]]出現前の古墳である。
[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）に[[発掘調査]]が行われ、舶載鏡の画文帯四獣鏡一面、銅族5本、[[直刀]]二口、鉄斧一個、鉇（やりがんな）一個、ガラス小玉数十個が出土した。前方部は一部削平されているが、後方部はほぼ原形をとどめている。全長64[[メートル]]、後方部長さ32メートル、幅約30メートル、高さ8メートル、前方部幅約16メートルである。後方部の中央の地表下から長さ3.2メートル、幅75[[センチメートル]]の木炭槨が確認され、同規模の[[割竹形木棺]]が使われたと推定されている。
墳丘には[[葺石]]が葺かれていた。後円部墳頂から古い様式の[[土師器]]が多数出土している。
[[1979年]]（昭和54年）[[3月13日]]、国指定史跡となった。その後、2002年（平成14年）12月19日「'''[[那須小川古墳群]]'''」として、本古墳と同形の[[吉田温泉神社古墳]]、[[那須八幡塚古墳]]とともに指定変更、名称変更が行われ一括して国の[[史跡]]に指定された。
現在、周囲は駒形公園として整備されている。

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=駒形大塚古墳 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=那須郡}} |所在地=[[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]小川 |所属=[[那須小川古墳群]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|45|42|N|140|7|14|E|region:JP-09_scale:20000}} |形状=[[前方後方墳]] |規模=全長61[[メートル|m]] 標高132.5m 幅28m |埋葬部=木炭槨 [[木棺]] |築造年代=4世紀中葉 |被葬者=梅曽の政府主であった韋提その人か、その系譜につながる[[国造]]の墓（推定） |出土品=鏡・鉄剣・[[鉄鏃]]・[[土器]]・[[刀子]]（小刀）・鉄斧・[[直刀]]・鉇 など |史跡指定=国の[[史跡]]（1979年3月13日指定） |特記事項= }}
'''駒形大塚古墳'''（こまがたおおつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]小川に位置する[[前方後方墳]]。
== 概要 == [[前方後円墳]]出現前の古墳である。
[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）に[[発掘調査]]が行われ、舶載鏡の画文帯四獣鏡一面、銅族5本、[[直刀]]二口、鉄斧一個、鉇（やりがんな）一個、ガラス小玉数十個が出土した。前方部は一部削平されているが、後方部はほぼ原形をとどめている。全長64[[メートル]]、後方部長さ32メートル、幅約30メートル、高さ8メートル、前方部幅約16メートルである。後方部の中央の地表下から長さ3.2メートル、幅75[[センチメートル]]の木炭槨が確認され、同規模の[[割竹形木棺]]が使われたと推定されている。
墳丘には[[葺石]]が葺かれていた。後円部墳頂から古い様式の[[土師器]]が多数出土している。
[[1979年]]（昭和54年）[[3月13日]]、本古墳が国の[[史跡]]に指定された。その後、2002年（平成14年）12月19日付けで本古墳と同形の[[吉田温泉神社古墳]]群、[[那須八幡塚古墳]]群が追加指定され、史跡としての名称は以上の3つの古墳（群）の総称である「'''[[那須小川古墳群]]'''」に変更された<ref>[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html 那須小川古墳群]（那珂川町サイト）</ref>。
現在、周囲は駒形公園として整備されている。

栃木県北東部、[[那珂川]]右岸の段丘上に築造された古墳である。現在までに前方部の一部が削平されているほか、[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘長は60.5メートル{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}（または64メートル{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}）を測る。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]が認められないほか、底部穿孔有段口辺壺が出土している{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。主体部の埋葬施設は後方部墳頂における木炭槨で、墳丘下1.5メートルにおいて長さ3.2メートル・幅0.75メートルを測り、同規模の割竹形木棺の埋葬が推定される{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。副葬品として、木炭槨からは画文帯四獣鏡1・銅鏃5・鉄製品（直刀2・鉄斧1・鉇1）・ガラス小玉数10が{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}、槨上からは土師器（高坏・器台・壺）が検出されている{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。 この駒形大塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]初頭頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}。那須地方では最古級の古墳であるとともに、栃木県ひいては東日本における古墳出現期の代表的な古墳として重要視される古墳である{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。 古墳域は[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定された。現在は駒形公園内で保存されている。また周辺では古墳数基が分布しており{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}、現存する2基は駒形大塚陪墳二号・三号として那珂川町指定史跡に指定されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）、発掘調査。副葬品出土{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 * [[1979年]]（昭和54年）3月13日、「駒形大塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定。 * [[2002年]]（平成14年）12月19日、既指定の史跡「駒形大塚古墳」に吉田温泉神社古墳群・那須八幡塚古墳群を追加指定して、指定名称を「那須小川古墳群」に変更（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち）。 File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|後方部墳頂 File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|前方部から後方部を望む File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後方部から前方部を望む ファイル:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|副葬品

1972年（昭和47年）9月29日指定[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1217-1721-52.html 町指定文化財（記念物）]（那珂川町ホームページ）。。 ** 駒形大塚陪墳三号 - 1972年（昭和47年）9月29日指定。 == 関連施設 == * 那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館（那珂川町小川） - 新宿||温泉
神社||[[吉田温泉神社古墳]]||50m||南||無||木炭槨 |- |八幡塚||[[那須八幡塚古墳]]||60.5m||西||有||木棺直葬 |}
栃木県北東部、[[那珂川]]右岸の段丘上に築造された古墳である。現在までに前方部の一部が削平されているほか、[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘長は60.5メートル{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}（または64メートル{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}）を測る。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]が認められないほか、底部穿孔有段口辺壺が出土している{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。主体部の埋葬施設は後方部墳頂における木炭槨で、墳丘下1.5メートルにおいて長さ3.2メートル・幅0.75メートルを測り、同規模の割竹形木棺の埋葬が推定される{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。副葬品として、木炭槨からは画文帯四獣鏡1・銅鏃5・鉄製品（直刀2・鉄斧1・鉇1）・ガラス小玉数10が{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}、槨上からは土師器（高坏・器台・壺）が検出されている{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。 この駒形大塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]初頭頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}。那須地方では最古級の古墳であるとともに、栃木県ひいては東日本における古墳出現期の代表的な古墳として重要視される古墳である{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。 古墳域は[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定された。現在は駒形公園内で保存されている。また周辺では古墳数基が分布しており{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}、現存する2基は駒形大塚陪墳二号・三号として那珂川町指定史跡に指定されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）、発掘調査。副葬品出土{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 * [[1979年]]（昭和54年）3月13日、「駒形大塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定。 * [[2002年]]（平成14年）12月19日、既指定の史跡「駒形大塚古墳」に吉田温泉神社古墳群・那須八幡塚古墳群を追加指定して、指定名称を「那須小川古墳群」に変更（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち）。 File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|後方部墳頂 File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|前方部から後方部を望む File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後方部から前方部を望む ファイル:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|副葬品

{{small|那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 駒形大塚古墳（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち） *: 1979年（昭和54年）3月13日、「駒形大塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定。 *: 2002年（平成14年）12月19日、既指定の史跡「駒形大塚古墳」に吉田温泉神社古墳群・那須八幡塚古墳群を追加指定して、指定名称を「那須小川古墳群」に変更{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}。 === 那珂川町指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 駒形大塚陪墳二号 - 1972年（昭和47年）9月29日指定[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1217-1721-52.html 町指定文化財（記念物）]（那珂川町ホームページ）。。 ** 駒形大塚陪墳三号 - 1972年（昭和47年）9月29日指定。 == 関連施設 == * 那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館（那珂川町小川） - 駒形大塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2003|chapter=|title= 国指定史跡 那須小川古墳群（小川町文化財資料集 第12冊）|publisher=小川町教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|那須小川古墳群|2003}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2013|chapter=|title=なす風土記ものがたり -なすの古墳をめぐって-（平成24年度 第1回企画展 大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館）|publisher=大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=駒形大塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=橋本澄朗|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 駒形大塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|駒形大塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E5%B0%8F%E5%B7%9D%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4-1444653 那須小川古墳群]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|那須小川古墳群（国指定史跡）}}}} - 古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height =300 | caption = {{center|那須地方の前方後方墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1=下侍塚 | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size1=20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2=上侍塚 | label-pos2=bottom | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size2=20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3=上侍塚北 | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size3=20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4=駒形大塚 | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size4=20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5=吉田温泉神社 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size5=20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6=那須八幡塚 | label-pos6=left | label-color6=black | mark6=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size6=20 }} '''駒形大塚古墳'''（こまがたおおつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]小川にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。[[那須小川古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「那須小川古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+那須地方の前方後方墳6基 !colspan=3|古墳群 !古墳名 !規模 !前方部 !葺石 !埋葬施設 |- |rowspan=3 colspan=3| ||[[下侍塚古墳]]||84m||南||有||木棺直葬 |- |[[上侍塚古墳]]||114m||南||有||[[粘土槨]] |- |[[上侍塚北古墳]]||48.5m||南|| ||不明 |- |rowspan=3|[[那須小川古墳群|那須
小川]]||colspan=2| ||'''駒形大塚古墳'''||60.5m||西||無||木炭槨 |- |rowspan=2|吉田
新宿||温泉
神社||[[吉田温泉神社古墳]]||50m||南||無||木炭槨 |- |八幡塚||[[那須八幡塚古墳]]||60.5m||西||有||木棺直葬 |}
栃木県北東部、[[那珂川]]右岸の段丘上に築造された古墳である。現在までに前方部の一部が削平されているほか、[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘長は60.5メートル{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}（または64メートル{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}）を測る。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]が認められないほか、底部穿孔有段口辺壺が出土している{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。埋葬施設は後方部墳頂における木炭槨で、墳丘下1.5メートルにおいて長さ3.2メートル・幅0.75メートルを測り、同規模の割竹形木棺の埋葬が推定される{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。副葬品として、木炭槨からは画文帯四獣鏡1・銅鏃5・鉄製品（直刀2・鉄斧1・鉇1）・ガラス小玉数10が{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}、槨上からは土師器（高坏・器台・壺）が検出されている{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]初頭頃と推定される{{Sfn|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}。那須地方では最古級の古墳であるとともに、栃木県ひいては東日本における古墳出現期の代表的な古墳として重要視される古墳である{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。 古墳域は[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定された。現在は駒形公園内で保存されている。また周辺では古墳数基が分布しており{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}、現存する2基は駒形大塚陪墳二号・三号として那珂川町指定史跡に指定されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）、発掘調査。副葬品出土{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 * [[1979年]]（昭和54年）3月13日、「駒形大塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定。 * [[2002年]]（平成14年）12月19日、既指定の史跡「駒形大塚古墳」に吉田温泉神社古墳群・那須八幡塚古墳群を追加指定して、指定名称を「那須小川古墳群」に変更（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち）。 File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|後方部墳頂 File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|前方部から後方部を望む File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後方部から前方部を望む ファイル:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|副葬品

1972年（昭和47年）9月29日指定[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1217-1721-52.html 町指定文化財（記念物）]（那珂川町ホームページ）。。 ** 駒形大塚陪墳三号 - 1972年（昭和47年）9月29日指定。 == 関連施設 == * 那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館（那珂川町小川） - 1972年（昭和47年）9月29日指定[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1217-1721-52.html 町指定文化財（記念物）]（那珂川町ホームページ）。。 ** 駒形大塚陪墳三号 - 1972年（昭和47年）9月29日指定。 == 関連施設 == * 那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館（那珂川町小川） - 駒形大塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2003|chapter=|title= 国指定史跡 那須小川古墳群（小川町文化財資料集 第12冊）|publisher=小川町教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|那須小川古墳群|2003}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2013|chapter=|title=なす風土記ものがたり -なすの古墳をめぐって-（平成24年度 第1回企画展 大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館）|publisher=大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=駒形大塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=橋本澄朗|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 駒形大塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|駒形大塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E5%B0%8F%E5%B7%9D%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4-1444653 那須小川古墳群]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|那須小川古墳群（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=|title=那須駒形大塚|publisher=小川町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[那須小川古墳群]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Komagata Otsuka Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}} * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html 那須小川古墳群]、[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/chizu/kuni/komagata.html 駒形大塚古墳] - {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 駒形大塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後方部） |別名 = |所属 = [[那須小川古墳群]] |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]小川（駒形公園内） |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 45|緯度秒 = 40.62 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 7|経度秒 = 15.42 |ISO = JP-09 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長60.5m |埋葬施設 = 木炭槨（内部に[[割竹形木棺]]?） |出土品 = 銅鏡・銅鏃・鉄製品・ガラス小玉 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]初頭 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「駒形大塚古墳」
（「[[那須小川古墳群]]」に包含） |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Tochigi |アイコン = 前方後方墳-西 |ラベル = 駒形大塚
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height =300 | caption = {{center|那須地方の前方後方墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1=下侍塚 | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size1=20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2=上侍塚 | label-pos2=bottom | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size2=20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3=上侍塚北 | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size3=20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4=駒形大塚 | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size4=20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5=吉田温泉神社 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size5=20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6=那須八幡塚 | label-pos6=left | label-color6=black | mark6=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size6=20 }} '''駒形大塚古墳'''（こまがたおおつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]小川にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。[[那須小川古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「那須小川古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+那須地方の前方後方墳6基 !colspan=3|古墳群 !古墳名 !規模 !前方部 !葺石 !埋葬施設 |- |rowspan=3 colspan=3| ||[[下侍塚古墳]]||84m||南||有||木棺直葬 |- |[[上侍塚古墳]]||114m||南||有||[[粘土槨]] |- |[[上侍塚北古墳]]||48.5m||南|| ||不明 |- |rowspan=3|[[那須小川古墳群|那須
小川]]||colspan=2| ||'''駒形大塚古墳'''||60.5m||西||無||木炭槨 |- |rowspan=2|吉田
新宿||温泉
神社||[[吉田温泉神社古墳]]||50m||南||無||木炭槨 |- |八幡塚||[[那須八幡塚古墳]]||60.5m||西||有||木棺直葬 |}
栃木県北東部、[[那珂川]]右岸の段丘上に築造された古墳である。現在までに前方部の一部が削平されているほか、[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘長は60.5メートル{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}（または64メートル{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}）を測る。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が認められないほか、底部穿孔有段口辺壺が出土している{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。埋葬施設は後方部墳頂における木炭槨で、墳丘下1.5メートルにおいて長さ3.2メートル・幅0.75メートルを測り、同規模の割竹形木棺の埋葬が推定される{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。副葬品として、木炭槨からは画文帯四獣鏡1・銅鏃5・鉄製品（直刀2・鉄斧1・鉇1）・ガラス小玉数10が{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}、槨上からは土師器（高坏・器台・壺）が検出されている{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]初頭頃と推定される{{Sfn|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}。那須地方では最古級の古墳であるとともに、栃木県ひいては東日本における古墳出現期の代表的な古墳として重要視される古墳である{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。 古墳域は[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定された。現在は駒形公園内で保存されている。また周辺では古墳数基が分布しており{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}、現存する2基は駒形大塚陪墳二号・三号として那珂川町指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）、発掘調査。副葬品出土{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 * [[1979年]]（昭和54年）3月13日、「駒形大塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定。 * [[2002年]]（平成14年）12月19日、既指定の史跡「駒形大塚古墳」に吉田温泉神社古墳群・那須八幡塚古墳群を追加指定して、指定名称を「那須小川古墳群」に変更（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち）。 File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|後方部墳頂 File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|前方部から後方部を望む File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後方部から前方部を望む ファイル:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|副葬品
{{small|那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館展示。}} ファイル:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|画文帯四獣鏡・土師器
{{small|那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館展示。}} ファイル:駒形大塚古墳出土 画文帯四獣鏡.JPG|画文帯四獣鏡
{{small|那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館展示。}} ファイル:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-3.JPG|土師器

{{Japanese Burial Mounds |Name = Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun (駒形大塚古墳) |Image = [[File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear section on the left back) |Alternate Names = |Group = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan (within Komagata Park) |Latitude = 36|Latitude_Minutes = 45|Latitude_Seconds = 40.62 |Longitude = 140|Longitude_Minutes = 7|Longitude_Seconds = 15.42 |ISO Code = JP-09 |Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} |Size = Mound Length: 60.5m |Burial Facility = Charcoal burial chamber (possibly {{ill|waritakegata mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirror, bronze arrowheads, iron items, glass beads |Mound Burial = None |Era = Early 4th century |Notable Features = Included in the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Historic Site designation |Tangible Cultural Property = |Special Features = |Map = Japan Tochigi |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |Label = Komagata Ōtsuka
Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays artifacts from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} [[Category:Burial Mounds in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakagawa Town, Tochigi]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]]

Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Four-Beast Belt Mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-3.JPG|Haniwa Pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun, part of {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}. ''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays artifacts from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} [[Category:Burial Mounds in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakagawa Town, Tochigi]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]]

Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Four-Beast Belt Mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-3.JPG|Haniwa Pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun, part of {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}. ''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays artifacts from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} [[Category:Burial Mounds in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakagawa Town, Tochigi]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]]

Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Four-Beast Belt Mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-3.JPG|Haniwa Pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun, part of {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}. ''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays artifacts from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} [[Category:Burial Mounds in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakagawa Town, Tochigi]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]]

Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Four-Beast Belt Mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-3.JPG|Haniwa Pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun, part of {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}. ''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays artifacts from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Four-Beast Belt Mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-3.JPG|Haniwa Pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun, part of {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}. ''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays artifacts from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods
