Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Four-Beast Belt Mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-3.JPG|Haniwa Pottery

''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays artifacts from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Four-Beast Belt Mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-3.JPG|Haniwa Pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun, part of {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}. ''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays artifacts from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} [[Category:Burial Mounds in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakagawa Town, Tochigi]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:前方後方墳]]

Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a stepped-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Four-Beast Belt Mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-3.JPG|Haniwa Pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun, part of {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}. ''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays artifacts from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} [[Category:Burial Mounds in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakagawa Town, Tochigi]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:pp]][[category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Komagata Ōtsuka Tumulus]][[Category:qq]]

Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a steqqed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a steqqed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

{{Japanese Burial Mounds |Name = Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun (駒形大塚古墳) |image = [[File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear section on the left back) |Alternate Names = |Group = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan (within Komagata Park) |Latitude = 36|Latitude_Minutes = 45|Latitude_Seconds = 40.62 |Longitude = 140|Longitude_Minutes = 7|Longitude_Seconds = 15.42 |ISO Code = JP-09 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Size = Mound Length: 60.5m |Burial Facility = Charcoal burial chamber (possibly {{ill|waritakegata mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirror, bronze arrowheads, iron items, glass beads |Mound Burial = None |Era = Early 4th century |Notable Features = Included in the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Historic Site designation |Tangible Cultural Property = |Special Features = |Map = Japan Tochigi |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |Label = Komagata Ōtsuka
Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a steqqed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby burial mounds are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Four-Beast Belt Mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-3.JPG|Haniwa Pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun, part of {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}. ''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays artifacts from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Japanese Kofuns |Name = Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun (駒形大塚古墳) |image = [[File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front section on the right, rear section on the left back) |Alternate Names = |Group = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan (within Komagata Park) |Latitude = 36|Latitude_Minutes = 45|Latitude_Seconds = 40.62 |Longitude = 140|Longitude_Minutes = 7|Longitude_Seconds = 15.42 |ISO Code = JP-09 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Size = Mound Length: 60.5m |Burial Facility = Charcoal burial chamber (possibly {{ill|waritakegata mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirror, bronze arrowheads, iron items, glass beads |Mound Burial = None |Era = Early 4th century |Notable Features = Included in the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Historic Site designation |Tangible Cultural Property = |Special Features = |Map = Japan Tochigi |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |Label = Komagata Ōtsuka
Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a steqqed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Four-Beast Belt Mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-3.JPG|Haniwa Pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun, part of {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}. ''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays artifacts from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Japanese Kofuns |Name = Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun (駒形大塚古墳) |image = [[File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the right, rear section on the left back) |Alternate Names = |Group = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan (within Komagata Park) |Latitude = 36|Latitude_Minutes = 45|Latitude_Seconds = 40.62 |Longitude = 140|Longitude_Minutes = 7|Longitude_Seconds = 15.42 |ISO Code = JP-09 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Size = Mound Length: 60.5m |Burial Facility = Charcoal burial chamber (possibly {{ill|waritakegata mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirror, bronze arrowheads, iron items, glass beads |Mound Burial = None |Era = Early 4th century |Notable Features = Included in the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Historic Site designation |Tangible Cultural Property = |Special Features = |Map = Japan Tochigi |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |Label = Komagata Ōtsuka
Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a steqqed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Four-Beast Belt Mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-3.JPG|Haniwa Pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun, part of {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}. ''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays artifacts from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a steqqed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

{{Japanese Kofuns |Name = Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun (駒形大塚古墳) |image = [[File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the right, rear section on the left back) |Alternate Names = |Group = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan (within Komagata Park) |Latitude = 36|Latitude_Minutes = 45|Latitude_Seconds = 40.62 |Longitude = 140|Longitude_Minutes = 7|Longitude_Seconds = 15.42 |ISO Code = JP-09 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Size = Mound Length: 60.5m |Burial Facility = Charcoal burial chamber (possibly {{ill|waritakegata mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirror, bronze arrowheads, iron items, glass beads |Mound Burial = None |Era = Early 4th century |Notable Features = Included in the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Historic Site designation |Tangible Cultural Property = |Special Features = |Map = Japan Tochigi |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |Label = Komagata Ōtsuka
Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a steqqed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Four-Beast Belt Mirror

''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays artifacts from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

Kofun (front section on the right, rear section on the left back) |Alternate Names = |Group = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan (within Komagata Park) |Latitude = 36|Latitude_Minutes = 45|Latitude_Seconds = 40.62 |Longitude = 140|Longitude_Minutes = 7|Longitude_Seconds = 15.42 |ISO Code = JP-09 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Size = Mound Length: 60.5m |Burial Facility = Charcoal burial chamber (possibly {{ill|waritakegata mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirror, bronze arrowheads, iron items, glass beads |Mound Burial = None |Era = Early 4th century |Notable Features = Included in the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Historic Site designation |Tangible Cultural Property = |Special Features = |Map = Japan Tochigi |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |Label = Komagata Ōtsuka
Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays artifacts from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

Kofun (front section on the right, rear section on the left back) |Alternate Names = |Group = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan (within Komagata Park) |Latitude = 36|Latitude_Minutes = 45|Latitude_Seconds = 40.62 |Longitude = 140|Longitude_Minutes = 7|Longitude_Seconds = 15.42 |ISO Code = JP-09 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Size = Mound Length: 60.5m |Burial Facility = Charcoal burial chamber (possibly {{ill|waritakegata mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}) |Artifacts = Bronze mirror, bronze arrowheads, iron items, glass beads |Mound Burial = None |Era = Early 4th century |Notable Features = Included in the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Historic Site designation |Tangible Cultural Property = |Special Features = |Map = Japan Tochigi |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |Label = Komagata Ōtsuka
Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

Kofun |Label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofuns |Name = Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun (駒形大塚古墳) |image = [[File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the right, rear section on the left back) |Alternate Names = |Group = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan (within Komagata Park) |Latitude = 36|Latitude_Minutes = 45|Latitude_Seconds = 40.62 |Longitude = 140|Longitude_Minutes = 7|Longitude_Seconds = 15.42 |ISO Code = JP-09 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Size = Mound Length: 60.5m |Burial Facility = Charcoal burial chamber (possibly {{ill|waritakegata mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirror, bronze arrowheads, iron items, glass beads |Mound Burial = None |Era = Early 4th century |Notable Features = Included in the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Historic Site designation |Tangible Cultural Property = |Special Features = |map = Japan Tochigi |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label = Komagata Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|Four-Beast Belt Mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-3.JPG|Haniwa Pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun, part of {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}. ''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays artifacts from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

Kofun (front section on the right, rear section on the left back) |Alternate Names = |part_of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun part_of|ja|那須小川古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan (within Komagata Park) |coordinates = 36|coordinates_Minutes = 45|coordinates_Seconds = 40.62 |coordinates = 140|coordinates_Minutes = 7|coordinates_Seconds = 15.42 |ISO Code = JP-09 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |area = Mound Length: 60.5m |Burial Facility = Charcoal burial chamber (possibly {{ill|waritakegata mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirror, bronze arrowheads, iron items, glass beads |Mound Burial = None |Era = Early 4th century |Notable Features = Included in the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Historic Site designation |Tangible Cultural Property = |Special Features = |map = Japan Tochigi |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label = Komagata Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun part_of|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods

Kofun (front section on the right, rear section on the left back) |Alternate Names = | part_of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan (within Komagata Park) |coordinates = 36|coordinates_Minutes = 45|coordinates_Seconds = 40.62 |coordinates = 140|coordinates_Minutes = 7|coordinates_Seconds = 15.42 |ISO Code = JP-09 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |area = Mound Length: 60.5m |Burial Facility = Charcoal burial chamber (possibly {{ill|waritakegata mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirror, bronze arrowheads, iron items, glass beads |Mound Burial = None |Era = Early 4th century |Notable Features = Included in the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Historic Site designation |Tangible Cultural Property = |Special Features = |map = Japan Tochigi |Icon = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label = Komagata Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | label-pos1 = Left | label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | label-pos2 = Bottom | label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | label-pos3 = Left | label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | label-pos4 = Top | label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | label-pos5 = Left | label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | label-pos6 = Left | label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and artifacts such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron artifacts, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was designated a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == Artifacts == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|Grave Goods
