So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofuns |name = Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun (駒形大塚古墳) |image = [[File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front section on the right, rear section on the left back) |alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|12=draft}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan (within Komagata Park) |coordinates = 36|coordinates_Minutes = 45|coordinates_Seconds = 40.62 |coordinates = 140|coordinates_Minutes = 7|coordinates_Seconds = 15.42 |omitted Code = JP-09 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} |area = Mound Length: 60.5m |burial = Charcoal burial chamber (possibly {{ill|waritakegata mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirror, bronze arrowheads, iron items, glass beads |Mound burial = None |built = Early 4th century |Notable Features = Included in the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Historic Site Historic Site |Tangible designation1 = |Special Features = |map = Japan Tochigi |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label = Komagata Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|12=draft}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Four-Beast Belt Mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-3.JPG|Haniwa Pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun, part of {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}. ''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国cultural property等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays burial goods from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofuns in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakagawa Town, Tochigi]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun}}

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 駒形大塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後方部） |別名 = |所属 = [[那須小川古墳群]] |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]小川（駒形公園内） |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 45|緯度秒 = 40.62 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 7|経度秒 = 15.42 |ISO = JP-09 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長60.5m |埋葬施設 = 木炭槨（内部に[[割竹形木棺]]?） |出土品 = 銅鏡・銅鏃・鉄製品・ガラス小玉 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]初頭 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「駒形大塚古墳」
（「[[那須小川古墳群]]」に包含） |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Tochigi |アイコン = 前方後方墳-西 |ラベル = 駒形大塚
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height =300 | caption = {{center|那須地方の前方後方墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1=下侍塚 | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size1=20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2=上侍塚 | label-pos2=bottom | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size2=20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3=上侍塚北 | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size3=20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4=駒形大塚 | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size4=20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5=吉田温泉神社 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size5=20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6=那須八幡塚 | label-pos6=left | label-color6=black | mark6=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size6=20 }} '''駒形大塚古墳'''（こまがたおおつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]小川にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。[[那須小川古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「那須小川古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+那須地方の前方後方墳6基 !colspan=3|古墳群 !古墳名 !規模 !前方部 !葺石 !埋葬施設 |- |rowspan=3 colspan=3| ||[[下侍塚古墳]]||84m||南||有||木棺直葬 |- |[[上侍塚古墳]]||114m||南||有||[[粘土槨]] |- |[[上侍塚北古墳]]||48.5m||南|| ||不明 |- |rowspan=3|[[那須小川古墳群|那須
小川]]||colspan=2| ||'''駒形大塚古墳'''||60.5m||西||無||木炭槨 |- |rowspan=2|吉田
新宿||温泉
神社||[[吉田温泉神社古墳]]||50m||南||無||木炭槨 |- |八幡塚||[[那須八幡塚古墳]]||60.5m||西||有||木棺直葬 |}
栃木県北東部、[[那珂川]]右岸の段丘上に築造された古墳である。現在までに前方部の一部が削平されているほか、[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘長は60.5メートル{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}（または64メートル{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}）を測る。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が認められないほか、底部穿孔有段口辺壺が出土している{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。埋葬施設は後方部墳頂における木炭槨で、墳丘下1.5メートルにおいて長さ3.2メートル・幅0.75メートルを測り、同規模の割竹形木棺の埋葬が推定される{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。副葬品として、木炭槨からは画文帯四獣鏡1・銅鏃5・鉄製品（直刀2・鉄斧1・鉇1）・ガラス小玉数10が{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}、槨上からは土師器（高坏・器台・壺）が検出されている{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]初頭頃と推定される{{Sfn|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}。那須地方では最古級の古墳であるとともに、栃木県ひいては東日本における古墳出現期の代表的な古墳として重要視される古墳である{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。 古墳域は[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定された。現在は駒形公園内で保存されている。また周辺では古墳数基が分布しており{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}、現存する2基は駒形大塚陪墳二号・三号として那珂川町指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）、発掘調査。副葬品出土{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 * [[1979年]]（昭和54年）3月13日、「駒形大塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定。 * [[2002年]]（平成14年）12月19日、既指定の史跡「駒形大塚古墳」に吉田温泉神社古墳群・那須八幡塚古墳群を追加指定して、指定名称を「那須小川古墳群」に変更（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち）。 File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|後方部墳頂 File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|前方部から後方部を望む File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後方部から前方部を望む ファイル:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|副葬品
{{small|那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館展示。}} ファイル:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|画文帯四獣鏡・土師器
{{small|那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館展示。}} ファイル:駒形大塚古墳出土 画文帯四獣鏡.JPG|画文帯四獣鏡
{{small|那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館展示。}} ファイル:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-3.JPG|土師器
{{small|那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 駒形大塚古墳（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち） *: 1979年（昭和54年）3月13日、「駒形大塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定。 *: 2002年（平成14年）12月19日、既指定の史跡「駒形大塚古墳」に吉田温泉神社古墳群・那須八幡塚古墳群を追加指定して、指定名称を「那須小川古墳群」に変更{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}。 === 那珂川町指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 駒形大塚陪墳二号 - 1972年（昭和47年）9月29日指定[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1217-1721-52.html 町指定文化財（記念物）]（那珂川町ホームページ）。。 ** 駒形大塚陪墳三号 - 1972年（昭和47年）9月29日指定。 == 関連施設 == * 那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館（那珂川町小川） - 駒形大塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2003|chapter=|title= 国指定史跡 那須小川古墳群（小川町文化財資料集 第12冊）|publisher=小川町教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|那須小川古墳群|2003}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2013|chapter=|title=なす風土記ものがたり -なすの古墳をめぐって-（平成24年度 第1回企画展 大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館）|publisher=大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=駒形大塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=橋本澄朗|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 駒形大塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|駒形大塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E5%B0%8F%E5%B7%9D%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4-1444653 那須小川古墳群]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|那須小川古墳群（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=|title=那須駒形大塚|publisher=小川町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[那須小川古墳群]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Komagata Otsuka Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}} * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html 那須小川古墳群]、[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/chizu/kuni/komagata.html 駒形大塚古墳] - 那珂川町ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:こまかたおおつかこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:那珂川町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:栃木県にある国指定の史跡]]

栃木県北東部、[[那珂川]]右岸の段丘上に築造された古墳である。現在までに前方部の一部が削平されているほか、[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘長は60.5メートル{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}（または64メートル{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}）を測る。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が認められないほか、底部穿孔有段口辺壺が出土している{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。埋葬施設は後方部墳頂における木炭槨で、墳丘下1.5メートルにおいて長さ3.2メートル・幅0.75メートルを測り、同規模の割竹形木棺の埋葬が推定される{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。副葬品として、木炭槨からは画文帯四獣鏡1・銅鏃5・鉄製品（直刀2・鉄斧1・鉇1）・ガラス小玉数10が{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}、槨上からは土師器（高坏・器台・壺）が検出されている{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]初頭頃と推定される{{Sfn|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}。那須地方では最古級の古墳であるとともに、栃木県ひいては東日本における古墳出現期の代表的な古墳として重要視される古墳である{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。 古墳域は[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定された。現在は駒形公園内で保存されている。また周辺では古墳数基が分布しており{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}、現存する2基は駒形大塚陪墳二号・三号として那珂川町指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）、発掘調査。副葬品出土{{Sfn|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 * [[1979年]]（昭和54年）3月13日、「駒形大塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定。 * [[2002年]]（平成14年）12月19日、既指定の史跡「駒形大塚古墳」に吉田温泉神社古墳群・那須八幡塚古墳群を追加指定して、指定名称を「那須小川古墳群」に変更（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち）。 File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|後方部墳頂 File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|前方部から後方部を望む File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後方部から前方部を望む ファイル:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-1.JPG|副葬品
{{small|那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館展示。}} ファイル:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-2.JPG|画文帯四獣鏡・土師器
{{small|那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館展示。}} ファイル:駒形大塚古墳出土 画文帯四獣鏡.JPG|画文帯四獣鏡
{{small|那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館展示。}} ファイル:駒形大塚古墳 出土品-3.JPG|土師器
{{small|那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 駒形大塚古墳（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち） *: 1979年（昭和54年）3月13日、「駒形大塚古墳」として国の史跡に指定。 *: 2002年（平成14年）12月19日、既指定の史跡「駒形大塚古墳」に吉田温泉神社古墳群・那須八幡塚古墳群を追加指定して、指定名称を「那須小川古墳群」に変更{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}。 === 那珂川町指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 駒形大塚陪墳二号 - 1972年（昭和47年）9月29日指定[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1217-1721-52.html 町指定文化財（記念物）]（那珂川町ホームページ）。。 ** 駒形大塚陪墳三号 - 1972年（昭和47年）9月29日指定。 == 関連施設 == * 那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館（那珂川町小川） - 駒形大塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2003|chapter=|title= 国指定史跡 那須小川古墳群（小川町文化財資料集 第12冊）|publisher=小川町教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|那須小川古墳群|2003}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2013|chapter=|title=なす風土記ものがたり -なすの古墳をめぐって-（平成24年度 第1回企画展 大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館）|publisher=大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=駒形大塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|駒形大塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=橋本澄朗|year=1989|chapter=大塚古墳 > 駒形大塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|駒形大塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E5%B0%8F%E5%B7%9D%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4-1444653 那須小川古墳群]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|那須小川古墳群（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=|title=那須駒形大塚|publisher=小川町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[那須小川古墳群]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Komagata Otsuka Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}} * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html 那須小川古墳群]、[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/chizu/kuni/komagata.html 駒形大塚古墳] - Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|12=draft}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

Kofun (front section on the right, rear section on the left back) |alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagawa, Tochigi|qq=}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan (within Komagata Park) |coordinates = 36|coordinates_Minutes = 45|coordinates_Seconds = 40.62 |coordinates = 140|coordinates_Minutes = 7|coordinates_Seconds = 15.42 |omitted Code = JP-09 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |area = Mound Length: 60.5m |burial = Charcoal burial chamber (possibly {{ill|waritakegata mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/waritakegata mokkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}}) |burial goods = Bronze mirror, bronze arrowheads, iron items, glass beads |Mound burial = None |built = Early 4th century |Notable Features = Included in the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Historic Site Historic Site |Tangible designation1 = |Special Features = |map = Japan Tochigi |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West |map_dot_label = Komagata Ōtsuka
Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagawa, Tochigi|qq=}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|qq=}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国cultural property等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays burial goods from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|駒形大塚古墳|version=99995087|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofuns in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakagawa Town, Tochigi]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]]

Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagawa, Tochigi|qq=}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|qq=}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国cultural property等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays burial goods from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|駒形大塚古墳|version=99995087|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofuns in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakagawa Town, Tochigi]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Komagata-Daizuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]]

Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]], a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Four-Beast Belt Mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-3.JPG|Haniwa Pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun, part of [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]]. ''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国cultural property等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays burial goods from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|駒形大塚古墳|version=99995087|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofuns in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Nakagawa Town, Tochigi]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:栃木県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:栃木県の古墳]]

It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]], a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]], a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-3.JPG|Haniwa Pottery

Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]], a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国cultural property等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays burial goods from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|駒形大塚古墳|version=99995087|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Nakagawa Town, Tochigi]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:那珂川町の歴史]] {{Commons category|Komagata Otsuka Kofun}}

Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]], a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-3.JPG|Haniwa Pottery

''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国cultural property等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays burial goods from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|駒形大塚古墳|version=99995087|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:那珂川町の歴史]] {{Commons category|Komagata Otsuka Kofun}}

It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国cultural property等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays burial goods from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|駒形大塚古墳|version=99995087|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:那珂川の Municipal History]] {{Commons category|Komagata Otsuka Kofun}}

Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]], a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国cultural property等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays burial goods from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|駒形大塚古墳|version=99995087|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] {{Commons category|Komagata Otsuka Kofun}}

Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]], a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]], a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]], a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]], a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun, part of [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]]. ''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国cultural property等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays burial goods from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|駒形大塚古墳|version=99995087|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] {{Commons category|Komagata Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11668363}}
