Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]], a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]], a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-3.JPG|Haniwa Pottery

''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国cultural property等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays burial goods from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|駒形大塚古墳|version=99995087|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] {{Commons category|Komagata Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11668363}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]], a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]], a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]], a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国cultural property等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays burial goods from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|駒形大塚古墳|version=99995087|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Komagata Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11668363}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|Nakagawa, Tochigi|WD=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa, Tochigi}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|WD=Q18337610|lt=Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Four-Beast Belt Mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-3.JPG|Haniwa Pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun, part of {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|WD=Q18337610|lt=Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster}}. ''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国cultural property等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays burial goods from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|駒形大塚古墳|version=99995087|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Komagata Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11668363}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|Nakagawa, Tochigi|WD=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa, Tochigi}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|WD=Q18337610|lt=Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-2.JPG|Four-Beast Belt Mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-3.JPG|Haniwa Pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum.}} == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun, part of {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|WD=Q18337610|lt=Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster}}. ''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国cultural property等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays burial goods from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|駒形大塚古墳|version=99995087|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Komagata Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11668363}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:pages]]

Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|Nakagawa, Tochigi|WD=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa, Tochigi|1=Nakagawa, Tochigi}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|WD=Q18337610|lt=Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|1=Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

''' Designated March 13, 1979{{国cultural property等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}. == Related Facilities == * Nakagawa Town Fudoki Hill Museum - houses and displays burial goods from the mound. == References == {{Reflist}} {{small|This content has been summarized using public domain material.}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|駒形大塚古墳|version=99995087|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:駒形大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Komagata Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11668363}} [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]

Kofun |map_dot_label_Position = Top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Zenpō-Kōhō-Fun in Nasu Area}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | map_dot_label1 = Shimosamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos1 = Left | map_dot_label-color1 = Black | mark1 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size1 = 20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | map_dot_label2 = Kamisamurai Mound | map_dot_label-pos2 = Bottom | map_dot_label-color2 = Black | mark2 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size2 = 20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | map_dot_label3 = Kamisamurai North Mound | map_dot_label-pos3 = Left | map_dot_label-color3 = Black | mark3 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size3 = 20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | map_dot_label4 = Komagata Ōtsuka | map_dot_label-pos4 = Top | map_dot_label-color4 = Black | mark4 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size4 = 20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | map_dot_label5 = Yoshida Onsen Shrine | map_dot_label-pos5 = Left | map_dot_label-color5 = Black | mark5 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-South.png | mark-size5 = 20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | map_dot_label6 = Nasu Hachiman Mound | map_dot_label-pos6 = Left | map_dot_label-color6 = Black | mark6 = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-West.png | mark-size6 = 20 }} '''Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun''' (駒形大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|Nakagawa, Tochigi|qid=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa, Tochigi|1=Nakagawa, Tochigi}}, Ogawa, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|en|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|qid=Q18337610|lt=Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|1=Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster}}, a national historic site. == Overview == Located on the right bank terrace of the Nakagawa River in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture, this mound has seen partial destruction of its front section. Excavations were conducted in 1974 (Showa 49){{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. The mound is zenpō-kōhō-fun-shaped and faces west. It measures 60.5 meters in length{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}} (or 64 meters in another estimate{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}). It does not feature stone-facing, and burial goods such as a ste11ed-rim jar with a pierced bottom were recovered{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. The burial chamber at the rear section summit is a charcoal burial pit measuring 3.2 meters in length and 0.75 meters in width, with evidence suggesting the use of a waritakegata mokkan{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Grave goods include a mirror, bronze arrowheads, and iron burial goods, as well as small glass beads{{Sfn|平凡社|1988}}. Haniwa pottery was found on top of the burial chamber{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods

This mound is one of the oldest examples of its type in the Nasu region, dating to the early 4th century, making it a key representative of early burial culture in Tochigi and Eastern Japan{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}. It was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1979 and is preserved in Komagata Park. Other nearby Kofuns are recognized as local historic sites. == burial goods == File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear Section Mound Top File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section to the rear File:Komagata Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section to the front File:駒形大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts-1.JPG|burial goods
